{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc 'cat .workflow/iphone-app-factory/spec.md'","aggregated_output":"# Product Spec

## Research Evidence
- Evidence inputs reviewed: App Store reviews, Reddit pain mining, competitor scans, iOS design-pattern synthesis, and the quality bar contract.
- Evidence confidence: medium-high. Signals are consistent across sources on reliability distrust, anti-autopilot need, ad friction, and relapse after dismissal.
- Primary target user: heavy sleepers and chronic snoozers facing real-world consequences (work, school, caregiving, shift transitions).
- Core job-to-be-done: convert `alarm fired` into `awake and executing first morning action`, not just dismissal.

## User Journeys
- `J-01 Critical Wake`: User creates a strict alarm with mission stack, alarm fires, user completes adaptive mission, passes wake-check, completes first micro-task, and sees reliability timeline entry.
- `J-02 Relapse Recovery`: User dismisses but ignores wake-check; app escalates (configured louder pattern / repeat challenge), requires re-validation, logs escalation outcome.
- `J-03 Trust Verification`: User opens reliability ledger and sees per-alarm timeline: scheduled, triggered, mission completion, wake-check status, escalation/failure reason.
- `J-04 Fast Setup`: New user completes setup wizard with iOS constraint disclosure, permission rationale, and privacy explanation before first alarm activation.

## MVP Scope
- In scope:
  - Alarm creation/editing with mission stack and strictness level.
  - Adaptive mission rotation across at least 3 modalities: cognitive, movement, scan/photo/object.
  - Post-dismiss wake-check contract with configurable window and escalation.
  - Reliability ledger with human-readable per-alarm execution logs.
  - First-task bridge: one user-selected micro-task required after wake verification.
  - Ad-free critical wake path (active alarm + immediate post-dismiss + wake-check).
  - Basic weekly wake consistency summary.
- Non-goals:
  - Sleep-stage tracking platform.
  - Public social feed or stranger accountability mechanics.
  - Wellness content library (stories/sounds/coaching).
  - Wearable-dependent launch requirements.

## Boilerplate Reuse
- Base: extend `SwiftAIBoilerplatePro` in `apps/waketask-iphone`; no platform rebuild.
- Mandatory policy: extend before replace.
- Rebuilds forbidden without ADR-level justification: auth, payments, AI, storage, networking, localization, settings, design system, DI/composition root.
- Any proposed net-new infrastructure must include:
  - Existing module evaluated.
  - Gap found.
  - Why extension is not viable.
  - Replacement cost/benefit and App Store 4.3 impact.

## Design Direction
- Product tone: calm, serious, high-trust; avoid gimmick and shame framing.
- UX priorities:
  - Single clear next action during active alarm.
  - Large controls, high legibility, high contrast, clear countdown/status.
  - No monetization interruptions in critical wake path.
  - Explicit privacy copy for on-device mission validation.
- Differentiation Contract (4.3 hardening):
  - `D-01` Reliability ledger is visible and usable in first session.
  - `D-02` Post-dismiss wake-check escalation is active and configurable.
  - `D-03` Adaptive anti-autopilot mission rotation changes mission pattern over time.
  - `D-04` First-task bridge is integrated into wake completion flow.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `FR-01 Alarm Setup`: User can create, edit, enable, disable alarms with mission stack and strictness.
  - Verification: UI test creates and edits alarm; persisted state reload confirms values.
- `FR-02 Mission Engine`: App supports at least 3 mission modalities and rotation logic.
  - Verification: unit tests validate modality selection and anti-repeat rules.
- `FR-03 Wake Contract`: Dismissal requires wake-check within configured window; missed check triggers escalation.
  - Verification: integration test with mocked time path asserts escalation state transition.
- `FR-04 Reliability Ledger`: Each alarm run writes timeline records with success/failure reason.
  - Verification: repository integration test asserts schema fields and ordering.
- `FR-05 First-Task Bridge`: Wake flow is incomplete until selected micro-task marked complete.
  - Verification: UI test blocks completion until first task done.
- `FR-06 Ad-Free Critical Path`: No interstitial ads in alarm, dismiss, wake-check flows.
  - Verification: release audit checklist plus automated string scan for ad-sdk entrypoints in critical flow module.
- `NFR-01 Performance`: cold launch p95 <= 2.0s on target simulator profile.
  - Verification: startup metric capture in CI report.
- `NFR-02 Interaction Latency`: mission interaction response p95 <= 200ms.
  - Verification: UI performance test with 30 sampled actions.
- `NFR-03 Stability`: crash-free sessions >= 99.5% in pre-release test runs.
  - Verification: Appium exploratory + XCTest run logs with crash count = 0 for gate pass.
- `NFR-04 Accessibility`: Dynamic Type, VoiceOver labels, contrast >= WCAG AA, tap targets >= 44x44 pt for core flows.
  - Verification: accessibility audit checklist + XCTest assertions where automatable.
- `NFR-05 Privacy/Data`: mission validation data processed on-device for MVP; no secret values logged.
  - Verification: code review plus log scan gate with zero violations.
- `NFR-06 File/Code Quality`: Swift production and test files remain <= 400 lines; Swift 6 strict concurrency preserved.
  - Verification: lint/build gate and line-count script in CI.

## Appium Exploratory QA
- Required artifact: `reports/ios/appium-exploratory-report.json`.
- Pass conditions:
  - Real simulator/Appium/XCUITest run executed.
  - `buttons_tapped > 0` and representative coverage of all reachable MVP screens.
  - `crashes = 0`, `failures = 0`.
- Required report fields:
  - `ok`, `buttons_tapped`, `screens_visited`, `crashes`, `failures`, `failures_detail`.
- Deferral policy:
  - For this run, deferral is not allowed: allow_macos_deferred=false.
  - Appium/XCUITest exploratory evidence must come from GitHub Actions hosted macOS iOS simulator evidence.
  - Local simulator limitations and human testing are not acceptance evidence tonight.
  - Missing Appium evidence blocks release handoff.

## App Store Readiness
- 4.3 anti-clone gate must pass:
  - Differentiation contract (`D-01`..`D-04`) is visible in onboarding, core wake flow, and metadata/screenshots narrative.
  - Release strings and metadata must not read as generic template alarm clone copy.
- Compliance and release evidence must include:
  - `xcodebuild build` pass.
  - `xcodebuild test` pass.
  - SwiftLint, SwiftFormat, Qlty, and secret scan all pass as blocking gates.
  - Privacy disclosures align with actual data behavior.
- Security handling:
  - Credential checks are true/false presence only.
  - No printing/logging/writing of secret values in artifacts.

## Definition of Done
- Canonical DoD is maintained in `.workflow/iphone-app-factory/definition-of-done.md` and is release-blocking.
- `Done` requires every checklist item to be verifiably satisfied with evidence artifacts.
","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc 'cat .workflow/iphone-app-factory/definition-of-done.md'","aggregated_output":"# Definition of Done

- [ ] `DoD-01` Scope lock: all shipped features map to spec MVP scope; non-goals are not implemented.
  - Evidence: scope trace in `.workflow/iphone-app-factory/spec-consensus.md`.
- [ ] `DoD-02` Requirement traceability: every `FR-*` and `NFR-*` maps to at least one test and one evidence artifact.
  - Evidence: trace matrix in `.workflow/iphone-app-factory/spec-consensus.md`.
- [ ] `DoD-03` Boilerplate reuse policy satisfied: no forbidden platform rebuilds without ADR justification.
  - Evidence: architecture/ADR review notes and changed-file audit.
- [ ] `DoD-04` Build gate passes on macOS lane.
  - Test: `xcodebuild build` succeeds.
- [ ] `DoD-05` Test gate passes on macOS lane.
  - Test: `xcodebuild test` succeeds.
- [ ] `DoD-06` Static quality gates pass.
  - Test: SwiftLint, SwiftFormat, Qlty, secret scan all green and blocking.
- [ ] `DoD-07` File size and language constraints pass.
  - Test: production and test Swift files <= 400 LOC; Swift 6 strict concurrency enforced.
- [ ] `DoD-08` Accessibility gate passes for core flows.
  - Test: Dynamic Type, VoiceOver labels, contrast checks, and 44x44 pt tap target verification.
- [ ] `DoD-09` Reliability features are functional.
  - Test: reliability ledger logs alarm timeline and failure reasons in integration tests.
- [ ] `DoD-10` Wake contract and escalation are functional.
  - Test: missed wake-check transitions to escalation path in automated tests.
- [ ] `DoD-11` Adaptive mission rotation is functional.
  - Test: automated tests validate modality coverage and anti-repeat behavior.
- [ ] `DoD-12` First-task bridge is functional.
  - Test: wake completion blocked until micro-task completion in UI test.
- [ ] `DoD-13` Appium exploratory QA pass is present.
  - Evidence: `reports/ios/appium-exploratory-report.json` with `ok=true`, `crashes=0`, `failures=0`.
- [ ] DoD-14 Appium deferral is forbidden for this run.
  - Rule: allow_macos_deferred=false; GitHub Actions hosted macOS iOS simulator Appium/XCUITest evidence is required, and missing evidence blocks release handoff.
- [ ] `DoD-15` App Store 4.3 anti-clone gate passes.
  - Test: differentiators `D-01..D-04` are present in onboarding, core flow, and store narrative.
- [ ] `DoD-16` Privacy and secret-handling policy passes.
  - Test: no hardcoded secrets, no credential values in logs/artifacts, only true/false presence checks.
- [ ] `DoD-17` Release evidence bundle is complete.
  - Evidence: CI quality report, Appium report, release-readiness notes, and final consensus verdict.
- [ ] `DoD-18` Final review fanout has no blocking issues.
  - Evidence: product fidelity, iOS architecture, security/privacy, code quality, QA evidence, and release readiness all approve.
